class.name: AutoworkInputSender
class.experimental: Some methods may not be implemented and may be changed in future versions.
class.brief_description: Simulates input events for automated testing.
class.description:
The AutoworkInputSender provides utilities to simulate user input during tests.
It allows triggering input actions, keyboard events, and mouse interactions either globally or targeted at a specific [Node]. This is useful for testing gameplay systems, UI interactions, and input-driven logic without requiring manual input.

method.name: action_down
method.description:
Simulates an input action press.
The specified [param action] is triggered as if it were pressed, with an optional [param strength] value. If [param target] is provided, the event is sent directly to that node.

method.name: action_up
method.description:
Simulates an input action release.
The specified [param action] is released. If [param target] is provided, the event is sent directly to that node.

method.name: key_down
method.description:
Simulates a key press event.
The given [param keycode] is sent as a key down event. If [param target] is provided, the event is directed to that node.
[codeblock] sender.key_down(KEY_SPACE) [/codeblock]

method.name: key_up
method.description:
Simulates a key release event.
The given [param keycode] is sent as a key up event. If [param target] is provided, the event is directed to that node.
[codeblock] sender.key_up(KEY_SPACE) [/codeblock]

method.name: mouse_down
method.description:
Simulates a mouse button press.
The specified mouse [param button_index] is pressed at the given [param position]. If [param target] is provided, the event is sent directly to that node.
[codeblock] sender.mouse_down(MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT, Vector2(100, 100)) [/codeblock]

method.name: mouse_motion
method.description:
Simulates mouse movement.
The cursor is moved to [param position], with an optional [param relative] motion vector. If [param target] is provided, the event is directed to that node.

method.name: mouse_up
method.description:
Simulates a mouse button release.
The specified mouse [param button_index] is released at the given [param position]. If [param target] is provided, the event is sent directly to that node.
[codeblock] sender.mouse_up(MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT, Vector2(100, 100)) [/codeblock]

method.name: reset_inputs
method.description:
Resets all simulated input states.
This clears any active or "pressed" inputs previously triggered by the sender, ensuring a clean state for subsequent tests.
